name: Miami: South Beach Art Deco Walk

stops.name: Colony Hotel
stops.shortDescription: Built in 1935, this is one of the most photographed Art Deco hotels on Ocean Drive. Its iconic blue neon sign frequently appears in movies and television shows depicting Miami Beach.

stops.name: Art Deco Welcome Center
stops.shortDescription: The headquarters of the Miami Design Preservation League. They were instrumental in saving the historic district's pastel-colored architecture from demolition in the 1970s.

stops.name: Lummus Park
stops.shortDescription: A lush, palm-fringed public park that separates the bustling Ocean Drive from the sandy beach. It serves as the lively, muscular playground for Miami's rollerbladers, volleyball players, and sunbathers.

stops.name: The Villa Casa Casuarina
stops.shortDescription: Formerly the opulent mansion of fashion designer Gianni Versace, who was tragically assassinated on its front steps in 1997. It is now a luxury boutique hotel featuring a stunning 24-karat gold-tiled swimming pool.

stops.name: Española Way
stops.shortDescription: A historic, pedestrian-only street designed in the 1920s to resemble romantic Mediterranean villages in Spain and France. Today, its pink stucco buildings house vibrant tapas restaurants and bohemian cafes.

stops.name: Lincoln Road Mall
stops.shortDescription: A premier outdoor pedestrian promenade designed in the 1950s by legendary architect Morris Lapidus. It stretches east to west across Miami Beach and offers world-class dining, shopping, and people-watching.

stops.name: Soundscape Park
stops.shortDescription: A stunning urban park featuring futuristic pergolas and a massive 7,000-square-foot outdoor projection wall where the New World Symphony broadcasts live concerts.

stops.name: Miami Beach Botanical Garden
stops.shortDescription: A lush, 2.6-acre subtropical garden oasis featuring an exquisite Japanese garden, native Florida plants, and tranquil water features right in the city center.

stops.name: Holocaust Memorial Miami Beach
stops.shortDescription: A striking, deeply moving monument featuring a giant bronze arm reaching toward the sky, surrounded by hauntingly detailed life-size statues depicting the victims.

stops.name: The Bass Museum of Art
stops.shortDescription: Miami Beach's contemporary art museum, housed in a magnificent 1930s Art Deco building in Collins Park. It features cutting-edge international exhibitions.

stops.name: Collins Park
stops.shortDescription: A beautiful cultural hub surrounded by museums and historic architecture. It often hosts massive, temporary public art installations, especially during Art Basel.

stops.name: Miami Beach Boardwalk
stops.shortDescription: A scenic, elevated wooden pathway running between the upscale oceanfront hotels and the beach. It's perfect for a breezy stroll with unobstructed Atlantic Ocean views.

stops.name: The Raleigh Hotel
stops.shortDescription: An Art Deco masterpiece famously known for its iconic, curvaceous swimming pool that Life Magazine once dubbed 'the most beautiful pool in America'.

stops.name: Delano South Beach
stops.shortDescription: A luxurious hotel that kickstarted the South Beach revival in the 90s, featuring stark white interiors designed by Philippe Starck and a highly exclusive poolside club.
